notes:
protondb ruins my spacing. Pretty format is at: https://gist.github.com/credomane/05a62924aa4ac7beb9376b1b82b7209a

There are three things are absolutely required to launch the game.

First your processor must support SSE4.1. You can check with Steam. Open steam -> Top Menu -> Help -> System Information. Verify under processor information that SSE41 says supported.
Second the Mafia III Launcher is borked and the real cause of all the borked reports. Rename the launcher.exe in the Mafia III folder to laucher_borked.exe. Then make a copy of mafia3.exe and name the copy launcher.exe.
Third fullscreen mode is completely borked in my experience and the game will launch to a black screen where it freezes. You have to create a custom videoconfig.cfg file with the contents "0 0 1280 720 0 0 0 0" with out the quotes to launch the game in windowed mode, 720p resolution and vsync disabled.

You create this file in a rather odd place due to proton. Browse out to the "root" folder of the steamlibrary where Mafia III is installed.
Then browse out to through this super long folder structure creating any folder required (steamapps/compatdata folders should exist already): {steamlibrary root}/steamapps/compatdata/360430/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/2K Games/Mafia III/Saves/

Also once you get in the game you can change display settings. However enabling vsync will slow the game down to 30fps (from my 200+fps with it off) and it plays like stop motion with it on, no joke, 0.1fps is more accurate. Enabling fullscreen is an instant black screen freeze forcing the videoconfig.cfg modification again.
title: Mafia III

notes: Apparently it work with some major tinkering, but it isn't mainlined yet. https://github.com/doitsujin/dxvk/issues/298
title: Mafia III

notes: Rename the launcher.exe in the Mafia III folder to laucher_borked.exe. Then make a copy of mafia3.exe and name the copy launcher.exe. Third fullscreen mode is completely borked in my experience and the game will launch to a black screen where it freezes. Go to {steamlibrary root}/steamapps/compatdata/360430/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/2K Games/Mafia III/Saves/ and change the line in videoconfig.cfg to "0 0 1280 720 0 0 0 0". Once you get in the game you can change display settings. Disable vsync as it makes the game super slow. Do not enable fullscreen, it is an instant black screen freeze forcing. Then the game worked quite well with DXVK.
title: Mafia III

cpu: AMD A10-5800K APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes: Replaced 'launcher.exe' with 'mafia3.exe' to get it to start (launcher is broken).  With D3d11 disabled: Menu is laggy but I managed to get ingame. Terrible quality, colors are off and everything is way too dark, less than 5 fps and often dropping to less than one fps. With D3d11 enabled (default): Menu works well, smooth mouse but once you want to go ingame you encounter endless loading. Game does run well on Windows at 20-50 fps.
title: Mafia III

notes: Runs very good. Sometimes starts micro stutter, but lasts for short time. HOWTO: rename launcher.exe to something else, and rename mafia3.exe to launcher.exe IF the game starts in black screen, then you need to edit videoconfig.cfg which is at .steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/360430/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/2K Games/Mafia III/Saves/ FROM 282 174 2560 1440 1 0 0 0 TO 0 0 2560 1440 0 0 0 0 it's basically needs to make game in windowed mode, so resolution numbers stay, just other numbers gets to 0. When game starts in Windowed mode, you can change settings. I recommend turn of VSync and make FPS Unlimited, otherwise performance is not that good. Played around 1hr, runs smooth. Graphics seems to be a bit different from Windows version as some of effects might be missing, but I'm unsure. Plays well
title: Mafia III

notes: The game runs after renaming mafia3.exe to launcher.exe. I have to play the game in windowed mode or it will freeze in the main menu or when a video cutscene starts playing. Performance wise the games runs quite horrible on the 390x, 25-30fps usually at 1440p high settings, tweaking settings makes little difference. The game has a few serious bugs, the most common one is that it has "lag spots". There are a few small areas in the game, always in buildings, if you stand in these areas the game will lag out and run at 1-3fps, you will often then also see a small rope-like graphical artifact somewhere nearby. If you leave the lag spot the lagging stops, if you go back in the lag spot for a second time the game will freeze. So you have to actively avoid these spots.
title: Mafia III
